title: PersonaBlueprint
description: v2. The distilled, IMMUTABLE spine of a persona. Substrate is Big Five / OCEAN via public-domain IPIP item pools (https://ipip.ori.org), used ONLY as a deterministic character-generation substrate, NOT validated clinical psychology (PLAN.md #11). No MBTI/16Personalities/NERIS marks, type names, or descriptions (legal hygiene). v2 adds provenance, wants/needs/wound, goals, knowledge, relationships, and an opening state, plus a richer voice. Everything beyond id+name is optional so v1 personas still validate, and a user can fill it in by hand to any depth. The mutable instance lives in persona_state.schema.json.

source_note.description: Provenance note. Engine never ships source text/logs; this references the user's own materials.

provenance.description: How this persona was built. Drives extraction and the consent/ethics posture.

input_types.description: One or more sources used to distill or author this persona.

consent_note.description: For a persona of a REAL person (e.g. from chat logs): who consented and to what. Ethics, not just legal.

ocean.description: Big Five / OCEAN as a generative substrate (IPIP, public domain). Each axis is a continuous position in [0,1], not a diagnosis.

facet_notes.description: Optional short notes on salient facets (e.g. 'high anxiety, low impulsiveness'). Not a diagnosis.

archetype.description: OPTIONAL discrete seed: our OWN named archetype from bucketing OCEAN. Name/description MUST be original (never any NERIS/MBTI text).

voice.description: How the persona speaks — the highest-value lever for sounding like them each turn.

register.description: e.g. 'terse and dry', 'warm and digressive', 'formal, archaic'.

speech_tics.description: Recurring verbal habits / fillers / catchphrases (paraphrased, not long verbatim).

exemplar_refs.description: RAG chunk ids exemplifying the voice. POINTERS only.

values.description: What the persona cares about / will defend or pursue.

wants.description: Surface desires — what they consciously pursue (character-bible 'want').

needs.description: Deeper needs they may not see — the 'need' beneath the want.

wound.description: The emotional wound / ghost that shapes behavior (character-bible).

goals.description: Active agenda the persona pursues in a scene (drives proactivity / salience).

knowledge.description: What the persona knows — and the epistemic limits the engine enforces.

epistemic_note.description: Hard limit: what this persona cannot know (epoch/role lock), e.g. 'a 19th-c. governess; no modern tech'.

relationships.description: Stances toward other people/personas. Enables persona-in-world (NPC relations).

boundaries.description: Consistency constraints the engine enforces (things the persona would not say/do).

history.description: Backstory seed that seeds long-term memory at instantiation.

opening.description: Initial mutable state for a new instance/conversation.

intro.description: Optional opening line / how they enter the scene.
